commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers include contributor real behavior proof and Gateway/security product approval, not just an automatable code edit.; Needs attention: The diff deliberately expands the Gateway security boundary to default-off host-FS read/write direct invoke and needs owner approval plus complete HTTP/RPC proof.; Review finding: [P2] Remove apply_patch from the schema commen... (sha=abe2394a12c131f51b74bf17d6cf5d6a51da2bc3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds dual-key `gateway.tools.directInvoke` opt-ins plus resolver, deny-list, audit, docs, and test wiring to expose `read`, `write`, and `edit` coding tools through Gateway direct-invoke HTTP/RPC surfaces.

PR surface: Source +291, Tests +207, Docs +105. Total +603 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, and the relevant verification is current-head HTTP/RPC proof for denied and enabled direct-invoke cases.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers include contributor real behavior proof and Gateway/security product approval, not just an automatable code edit.

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends PR #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` via the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver). Same dual-key gating shape as #85664 — distinct surface-specific opt-in flag PLUS per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` — so existing configs that already list `"write"` or `"edit"` in `allow` for unrelated surfaces (e.g. an MCP/agent surface) stay inert at the direct-invoke layer.

**This PR is the redesigned narrow write extension.** The prior 5-commit history was reset to a single fresh commit on top of #85664's `f31c56eeaf` — no rebase conflicts, just a clean extension that satisfies ClawSweeper's [P1] from the prior review.

## Dual-key gating (mirrors #85664's pattern)

To enable write-class tools on the direct-invoke surface, operators must set BOTH:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | **NEW.** Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. Without this, no write tool is materialized into the candidate set even if `tools.allow` includes its name. | `false` (omitted) |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` | Per-tool names the operator wants enabled. Subset OK — e.g. `["edit"]` alone enables only `edit`. | not present |

**Either key alone leaves write tools unreachable.** Old configs with `allow: ["write"]` (for an unrelated surface) stay inert.

Truth table (per tool name `T` ∈ `{write, edit}`):

| `tools.allow` includes `T` | `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` | `T` reachable on direct-invoke |
|---|---|---|
| no  | no   | ❌ |
| yes | no   | ❌ (tool not materialized) |
| no  | yes  | ❌ (filtered by HTTP deny) |
| yes | yes  | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (via `hostFsWrite: true` opt-in).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` opt-in (unchanged regression test).
- **`apply_patch`:** added to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for future-proofing, but NOT currently produced by the coding tool factory — deferred until upstream adds a factory entry.
- **NOT exposed (deferred):** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell`. These are RCE-class and require a separate owner/admin enforcement model — `operator.write` scope alone is not sufficient. Distinct follow-up PR.

## Files changed (delta against #85664)

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` (apply_patch already present upstream)
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — extends the existing read materialization block to also include write/edit when `hostFsWrite=true`
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` finding (only fires when both gates set + at least one write tool name in allow; silent otherwise)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — 6 new test cases covering all gate combinations + cross-class isolation (hostFsWrite + only read in allow → silent)
- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` — operator-facing dual-key opt-in docs + truth table + explicit note about deferred exec/process/spawn/shell

## Real-behavior proof on head `f31c56eeaf`

**Build:** `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs`. Output: `dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js` (contains `hostFsWrite`) and `dist/audit-DHrX1aSk.js` (contains `host_write_allow`).

**Driver:** Node ESM script imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from the freshly-compiled bundle, calls it with 10 config shapes covering: read regression (4 cases mirroring #85664), write dual-key gating (4 cases), mixed read+write (1 case), and an RCE-attempt case proving exec/process/spawn/shell never materialize even with all gates set.

```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[R-VANILLA]                                read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[R-ALLOW-ONLY  allow=["read"]]             read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[R-FLAG-ONLY   hostFsRead=true]            read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[R-BOTH        allow+flag]                 read=✓ write=  total=24  ✅
[W-ALLOW-ONLY  allow=write,edit,apply_p]   read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[W-FLAG-ONLY   hostFsWrite=true]           read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[W-BOTH-FULL   allow+flag]                 read=  write=✓ edit=✓    ✅
[W-BOTH-SUBSET allow=[edit] + flag]        read=  write=  edit=✓    ✅
[M-FULL        read+write allow+both flags] read=✓ write=✓ edit=    ✅
[RCE-ATTEMPT   allow=exec,process,shell,spawn + both flags]
                                            exec= process= shell= spawn=  ✅

=== ALL PASS === (10/10)
```

**Key results:**

- **W-BOTH-FULL** ✅ — both keys set + all write names in allow → `write` and `edit` are reachable. (`apply_patch` correctly absent — factory doesn't produce it yet, deferred for future.)
- **W-BOTH-SUBSET** ✅ — `allow: ["edit"]` + `hostFsWrite: true` → only `edit` reachable; `write` correctly absent because not in allow. Per-tool granularity verified.
- **W-ALLOW-ONLY** ✅ — bot-P1 critical case: legacy config with `allow: ["write", "edit", "apply_patch"]` alone does NOT grant write access on direct-invoke. The new `hostFsWrite: true` opt-in is required additionally.
- **RCE-ATTEMPT** ✅ — even with `allow: ["exec", "process", "shell", "spawn"]` AND both `hostFsRead` + `hostFsWrite` set, the RCE-class tools NEVER appear in the candidate set. This is structural (the materialization block only includes coding tool names, never RCE tools), not policy-filtered.

## Code-level test coverage

`src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` (new `describe` block for host_write_allow):

- ✅ fires (warn) when both keys set + `allow: ["write"]` on loopback
- ✅ fires for `allow: ["edit", "apply_patch"]` + `hostFsWrite: true`
- ✅ escalates to critical when bind is lan
- ✅ does NOT fire when only `allow: ["write"]` (legacy shape)
- ✅ does NOT fire when only `hostFsWrite: true`
- ✅ does NOT fire when `hostFsWrite: true` + `allow: ["read"]` (cross-class — different opt-in needed)

## Why a single fresh commit (not rebase of prior 5)

The original PR's 5 commits implemented a single-key `allow: ["exec", "process"]` opt-in with an `ownerOnly: true` metadata tag that the bot correctly flagged as DEAD CODE after upstream commit `02182d5a3031` removed the owner-only enforcement helper. Patching on top of those 5 commits to fix the bot's [P1] would have layered design-on-design. Cleaner to reset to #85664's head and write the redesign as one commit.

Backup of prior PR history retained at `backup/63919-pre-rebase-20260603-onto-85664` on the public fork.

## Why exec/process/spawn/shell are deferred

The bot's prior [P1] on this PR ("Enforce the exec/process owner gate") flagged that with upstream's removal of sender-owner gating, an `ownerOnly: true` tag is metadata-only — a trusted-proxy caller with `operator.write` can still reach allowlisted `exec`/`process` without owner check.

A `directInvoke.commandExec: true` flag (analogous to `hostFsWrite`) is **not sufficient** for the RCE-class because:
- `operator.write` is broadly distributed in trusted-proxy setups
- RCE-class needs `operator.admin` or `senderIsOwner` enforcement at the direct-invoke layer
- This requires threading principal context through `invokeGatewayTool` in `tools-invoke-shared.ts` — a separate refactor

That refactor belongs in its own PR with its own review surface. This PR ships the safe write extension and explicitly documents the deferral.

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Extend #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` over Gateway direct-invoke surfaces, using the same dual-key gating pattern (`gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` plus per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow`). Addresses ClawSweeper [P1] on the prior PR head (the dead `ownerOnly` tag); RCE-class tools (exec/process/spawn/shell) remain unavailable and are deferred to a follow-up PR.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `git checkout f31c56eeaf && docker run --rm -v $PWD:/work -w /work --platform linux/arm64 --memory=12g node:22-bookworm-slim bash -c 'set -e; corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@11.2.2 --activate && node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs && node /work/.proof-driver-63919.mjs'` — exits 0 with `=== ALL PASS ===` (10/10).

**Real environment tested:** built fresh from current PR head `f31c56eeaf` inside a clean throwaway `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container; workspace bind-mounted; produced `dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js`. Driver imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from that bundle and probes 10 config combinations.

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture, redacted):**
```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[R-VANILLA: no read keys]
  config: {}
  coding-tools present: []
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 23

[R-BOTH: allow:['read'] + hostFsRead:true]
  config: {"allow":["read"],"hostFsRead":true}
  coding-tools present: [read]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 24

[W-BOTH-FULL: allow:['write','edit','apply_patch'] + hostFsWrite:true]
  config: {"allow":["write","edit","apply_patch"],"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [write, edit]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 25

[W-BOTH-SUBSET: allow:['edit'] + hostFsWrite:true (only edit reachable)]
  config: {"allow":["edit"],"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [edit]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 24

[M-FULL: allow:['read','write'] + both flags]
  config: {"allow":["read","write"],"hostFsRead":true,"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [read, write]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 25

[RCE-ATTEMPT: allow:['exec','process','shell','spawn'] + both flags]
  config: {"allow":["exec","process","shell","spawn"],"hostFsRead":true,"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: []
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 23

=== ALL PASS === (10/10)
```

**Observed result after fix (head `f31c56eeaf`):** All 10 cases PASS. The bot's prior [P1] dead-code concern is structurally addressed by the new design: write-class tools are dual-key gated; RCE-class tools are explicitly NOT exposed (and the proof verifies they cannot leak even when an operator deliberately tries with all gates open).

**What was not tested:** Live HTTP request through `handleToolsInvokeHttp` to a running gateway. The `resolveGatewayScopedTools` driver tests the candidate-set construction (where the dual-key gating + structural RCE non-exposure live); downstream policy filter + HTTP dispatch are upstream-tested code paths.




## Live HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` proof

Addresses ClawSweeper's [P1]: "Add redacted current-head HTTP and SDK RPC proof for denied and enabled read/write/edit cases."

Built `dist/` fresh from head `f31c56eeaf` in `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, started the gateway via `node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 18900 --auth token --token <secret>`, mutated `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` per case, and POST'd `/tools/invoke` with node fetch targeting sentinel files. Stop + restart between cases.

**Results — 6 / 6 PASS:**

```
====================================================
LIVE HTTP /tools/invoke proof for PR #63919 (write/edit)
head: f31c56eeaf
====================================================

R-CASE 4 (regression): read with both keys
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["read"] hostFsRead=true hostFsWrite=false
  HTTP 200 body: {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PROOF READ source for #63919\n"}]}}

W-CASE 1: neither write gate (default-deny)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=[] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=false
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

W-CASE 2: only allow=[write] (legacy)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["write"] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=false
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

W-CASE 3: only hostFsWrite=true
  [mutate_cfg] allow=[] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=true
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

W-CASE 4: BOTH (success expected)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["write","edit"] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=true
  HTTP 200 body: {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 22 bytes to /tmp/proof-write-target.txt"}]}}

RCE-CASE: exec with all flags + allow=[exec]
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["exec"] hostFsRead=true hostFsWrite=true
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: exec"}}

write target file contents (proves W-CASE 4 actually wrote):
PROOF WRITE for #63919
```

**Critical observations:**

- **W-CASE 2** (legacy `allow:["write"]` only) returns HTTP **404 "Tool not available: write"** — same as the default-deny W-CASE 1. The dual-key gating prevents the bot's [P1] concern: existing configs that already include `"write"` in `allow` (for an unrelated surface) stay inert here, no silent host-FS write grant on upgrade.
- **W-CASE 4** (BOTH `hostFsWrite: true` + `allow:["write","edit"]`) returns HTTP **200** AND **actually wrote** the file (`PROOF WRITE for #63919` is the verbatim file content after the request).
- **RCE-CASE** demonstrates structural RCE non-exposure: even with `allow:["exec"]` PLUS both opt-in flags set, `exec` returns HTTP **404 "Tool not available: exec"**. The resolver does not materialize RCE-class tools into the direct-invoke candidate set; this is structural, not policy-filtered. Deferred to a follow-up PR with proper owner-gate enforcement.

## apply_patch alignment (ClawSweeper [P2])

Resolved the docs/audit/resolver mismatch flagged by ClawSweeper:

- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md`: truth table now lists `{write, edit}` only; explicit note that `apply_patch` is in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for future-proofing but the coding tool factory does not produce it for direct-invoke yet.
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts`: `WRITE_CLASS_TOOLS` reduced to `{write, edit}` (was `{write, edit, apply_patch}`). The `host_write_allow` audit no longer falsely claims host-write exposure for an inert `allow: ["apply_patch"]` entry.

`apply_patch` can be added in a follow-up PR when upstream wires the factory entry.

## Docs discoverability (ClawSweeper [P2])

Same surfaces as #85664, extended for `hostFsWrite`:

- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — FIELD_HELP entries for `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead` AND `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite`.
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` — paragraph for each opt-in, with audit-warning hookup.
- `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — catalog rows for `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_read_allow` AND `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow`.
target.author: simonusa

target.body:
Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends PR #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` via the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver). Same dual-key gating shape as #85664 — distinct surface-specific opt-in flag PLUS per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` — so existing configs that already list `"write"` or `"edit"` in `allow` for unrelated surfaces (e.g. an MCP/agent surface) stay inert at the direct-invoke layer.

**This PR is the redesigned narrow write extension.** The prior 5-commit history was reset to a single fresh commit on top of #85664's `f31c56eeaf` — no rebase conflicts, just a clean extension that satisfies ClawSweeper's [P1] from the prior review.

## Dual-key gating (mirrors #85664's pattern)

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | **NEW.** Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. Without this, no write tool is materialized into the candidate set even if `tools.allow` includes its name. | `false` (omitted) |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` | Per-tool names the operator wants enabled. Subset OK — e.g. `["edit"]` alone enables only `edit`. | not present |

**Either key alone leaves write tools unreachable.** Old configs with `allow: ["write"]` (for an unrelated surface) stay inert.

Truth table (per tool name `T` ∈ `{write, edit}`):

| `tools.allow` includes `T` | `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` | `T` reachable on direct-invoke |
|---|---|---|
| no  | no   | ❌ |
| yes | no   | ❌ (tool not materialized) |
| no  | yes  | ❌ (filtered by HTTP deny) |
| yes | yes  | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (via `hostFsWrite: true` opt-in).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` opt-in (unchanged regression test).
- **`apply_patch`:** added to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for future-proofing, but NOT currently produced by the coding tool factory — deferred until upstream adds a factory entry.
- **NOT exposed (deferred):** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell`. These are RCE-class and require a separate owner/admin enforcement model — `operator.write` scope alone is not sufficient. Distinct follow-up PR.

## Files changed (delta against #85664)

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` (apply_patch already present upstream)
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — extends the existing read materialization block to also include write/edit when `hostFsWrite=true`
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` finding (only fires when both gates set + at least one write tool name in allow; silent otherwise)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — 6 new test cases covering all gate combinations + cross-class isolation (hostFsWrite + only read in allow → silent)
- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` — operator-facing dual-key opt-in docs + truth table + explicit note about deferred exec/process/spawn/shell

**Build:** `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs`. Output: `dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js` (contains `hostFsWrite`) and `dist/audit-DHrX1aSk.js` (contains `host_write_allow`).

```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[R-VANILLA]                                read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[R-ALLOW-ONLY  allow=["read"]]             read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[R-FLAG-ONLY   hostFsRead=true]            read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[R-BOTH        allow+flag]                 read=✓ write=  total=24  ✅
[W-ALLOW-ONLY  allow=write,edit,apply_p]   read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[W-FLAG-ONLY   hostFsWrite=true]           read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[W-BOTH-FULL   allow+flag]                 read=  write=✓ edit=✓    ✅
[W-BOTH-SUBSET allow=[edit] + flag]        read=  write=  edit=✓    ✅
[M-FULL        read+write allow+both flags] read=✓ write=✓ edit=    ✅
[RCE-ATTEMPT   allow=exec,process,shell,spawn + both flags]
                                            exec= process= shell= spawn=  ✅

```

- **W-BOTH-SUBSET** ✅ — `allow: ["edit"]` + `hostFsWrite: true` → only `edit` reachable; `write` correctly absent because not in allow. Per-tool granularity verified.

- ✅ fires (warn) when both keys set + `allow: ["write"]` on loopback

- ✅ escalates to critical when bind is lan

- ✅ does NOT fire when only `hostFsWrite: true`
- ✅ does NOT fire when `hostFsWrite: true` + `allow: ["read"]` (cross-class — different opt-in needed)

- RCE-class needs `operator.admin` or `senderIsOwner` enforcement at the direct-invoke layer

That refactor belongs in its own PR with its own review surface. This PR ships the safe write extension and explicitly documents the deferral.

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Extend #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` over Gateway direct-invoke surfaces, using the same dual-key gating pattern (`gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` plus per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow`). Addresses ClawSweeper [P1] on the prior PR head (the dead `ownerOnly` tag); RCE-class tools (exec/process/spawn/shell) remain unavailable and are deferred to a follow-up PR.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `git checkout f31c56eeaf && docker run --rm -v $PWD:/work -w /work --platform linux/arm64 --memory=12g node:22-bookworm-slim bash -c 'set -e; corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@11.2.2 --activate && node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs && node /work/.proof-driver-63919.mjs'` — exits 0 with `=== ALL PASS ===` (10/10).

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture, redacted):**
```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[R-VANILLA: no read keys]
  config: {}
  coding-tools present: []
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 23

[R-BOTH: allow:['read'] + hostFsRead:true]
  config: {"allow":["read"],"hostFsRead":true}
  coding-tools present: [read]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 24

[W-BOTH-FULL: allow:['write','edit','apply_patch'] + hostFsWrite:true]
  config: {"allow":["write","edit","apply_patch"],"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [write, edit]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 25

[W-BOTH-SUBSET: allow:['edit'] + hostFsWrite:true (only edit reachable)]
  config: {"allow":["edit"],"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [edit]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 24

[M-FULL: allow:['read','write'] + both flags]
  config: {"allow":["read","write"],"hostFsRead":true,"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [read, write]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 25

[RCE-ATTEMPT: allow:['exec','process','shell','spawn'] + both flags]
  config: {"allow":["exec","process","shell","spawn"],"hostFsRead":true,"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: []
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 23

=== ALL PASS === (10/10)
```

**Observed result after fix (head `f31c56eeaf`):** All 10 cases PASS. The bot's prior [P1] dead-code concern is structurally addressed by the new design: write-class tools are dual-key gated; RCE-class tools are explicitly NOT exposed (and the proof verifies they cannot leak even when an operator deliberately tries with all gates open).

**What was not tested:** Live HTTP request through `handleToolsInvokeHttp` to a running gateway. The `resolveGatewayScopedTools` driver tests the candidate-set construction (where the dual-key gating + structural RCE non-exposure live); downstream policy filter + HTTP dispatch are upstream-tested code paths.




## Live HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` proof

Addresses ClawSweeper's [P1]: "Add redacted current-head HTTP and SDK RPC proof for denied and enabled read/write/edit cases."

Built `dist/` fresh from head `f31c56eeaf` in `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, started the gateway via `node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 18900 --auth token --token <secret>`, mutated `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` per case, and POST'd `/tools/invoke` with node fetch targeting sentinel files. Stop + restart between cases.

**Results — 6 / 6 PASS:**

```
====================================================
LIVE HTTP /tools/invoke proof for PR #63919 (write/edit)
head: f31c56eeaf
====================================================

R-CASE 4 (regression): read with both keys
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["read"] hostFsRead=true hostFsWrite=false
  HTTP 200 body: {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PROOF READ source for #63919\n"}]}}

W-CASE 1: neither write gate (default-deny)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=[] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=false
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

W-CASE 2: only allow=[write] (legacy)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["write"] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=false
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

W-CASE 3: only hostFsWrite=true
  [mutate_cfg] allow=[] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=true
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

W-CASE 4: BOTH (success expected)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["write","edit"] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=true
  HTTP 200 body: {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 22 bytes to /tmp/proof-write-target.txt"}]}}

RCE-CASE: exec with all flags + allow=[exec]
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["exec"] hostFsRead=true hostFsWrite=true
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: exec"}}

write target file contents (proves W-CASE 4 actually wrote):
PROOF WRITE for #63919
```

**Critical observations:**

- **W-CASE 2** (legacy `allow:["write"]` only) returns HTTP **404 "Tool not available: write"** — same as the default-deny W-CASE 1. The dual-key gating prevents the bot's [P1] concern: existing configs that already include `"write"` in `allow` (for an unrelated surface) stay inert here, no silent host-FS write grant on upgrade.
- **W-CASE 4** (BOTH `hostFsWrite: true` + `allow:["write","edit"]`) returns HTTP **200** AND **actually wrote** the file (`PROOF WRITE for #63919` is the verbatim file content after the request).
- **RCE-CASE** demonstrates structural RCE non-exposure: even with `allow:["exec"]` PLUS both opt-in flags set, `exec` returns HTTP **404 "Tool not available: exec"**. The resolver does not materialize RCE-class tools into the direct-invoke candidate set; this is structural, not policy-filtered. Deferred to a follow-up PR with proper owner-gate enforcement.

## apply_patch alignment (ClawSweeper [P2])

Resolved the docs/audit/resolver mismatch flagged by ClawSweeper:

- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md`: truth table now lists `{write, edit}` only; explicit note that `apply_patch` is in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for future-proofing but the coding tool factory does not produce it for direct-invoke yet.
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts`: `WRITE_CLASS_TOOLS` reduced to `{write, edit}` (was `{write, edit, apply_patch}`). The `host_write_allow` audit no longer falsely claims host-write exposure for an inert `allow: ["apply_patch"]` entry.

`apply_patch` can be added in a follow-up PR when upstream wires the factory entry.

## Docs discoverability (ClawSweeper [P2])

Same surfaces as #85664, extended for `hostFsWrite`:

- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — FIELD_HELP entries for `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead` AND `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite`.
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` — paragraph for each opt-in, with audit-warning hookup.
- `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — catalog rows for `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_read_allow` AND `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow`.
target.author: simonusa

commands.author: simonusa

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends PR #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` via the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the resolver). Same dual-key gating shape as #85664 — distinct surface-specific opt-in flag PLUS per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` — so existing configs that already list `"write"` or `"edit"` in `allow` for unrelated surfaces (e.g. an MCP/agent surface) stay inert at the direct-invoke layer.

**This PR is the redesigned narrow write extension.** The prior 5-commit history was reset to a single fresh commit on top of #85664's `f31c56eeaf` — no rebase conflicts, just a clean extension that satisfies ClawSweeper's [P1] from the prior review.

## Dual-key gating (mirrors #85664's pattern)

To enable write-class tools on the direct-invoke surface, operators must set BOTH:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | **NEW.** Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. Without this, no write tool is materialized into the candidate set even if `tools.allow` includes its name. | `false` (omitted) |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` | Per-tool names the operator wants enabled. Subset OK — e.g. `["edit"]` alone enables only `edit`. | not present |

**Either key alone leaves write tools unreachable.** Old configs with `allow: ["write"]` (for an unrelated surface) stay inert.

Truth table (per tool name `T` ∈ `{write, edit}`):

| `tools.allow` includes `T` | `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` | `T` reachable on direct-invoke |
|---|---|---|
| no  | no   | ❌ |
| yes | no   | ❌ (tool not materialized) |
| no  | yes  | ❌ (filtered by HTTP deny) |
| yes | yes  | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (via `hostFsWrite: true` opt-in).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` opt-in (unchanged regression test).
- **`apply_patch`:** added to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for future-proofing, but NOT currently produced by the coding tool factory — deferred until upstream adds a factory entry.
- **NOT exposed (deferred):** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell`. These are RCE-class and require a separate owner/admin enforcement model — `operator.write` scope alone is not sufficient. Distinct follow-up PR.

## Files changed (delta against #85664)

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` (apply_patch already present upstream)
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — extends the existing read materialization block to also include write/edit when `hostFsWrite=true`
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` finding (only fires when both gates set + at least one write tool name in allow; silent otherwise)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — 6 new test cases covering all gate combinations + cross-class isolation (hostFsWrite + only read in allow → silent)
- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` — operator-facing dual-key opt-in docs + truth table + explicit note about deferred exec/process/spawn/shell

## Real-behavior proof on head `f31c56eeaf`

**Build:** `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs`. Output: `dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js` (contains `hostFsWrite`) and `dist/audit-DHrX1aSk.js` (contains `host_write_allow`).

**Driver:** Node ESM script imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from the freshly-compiled bundle, calls it with 10 config shapes covering: read regression (4 cases mirroring #85664), write dual-key gating (4 cases), mixed read+write (1 case), and an RCE-attempt case proving exec/process/spawn/shell never materialize even with all gates set.

```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[R-VANILLA]                                read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[R-ALLOW-ONLY  allow=["read"]]             read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[R-FLAG-ONLY   hostFsRead=true]            read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[R-BOTH        allow+flag]                 read=✓ write=  total=24  ✅
[W-ALLOW-ONLY  allow=write,edit,apply_p]   read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[W-FLAG-ONLY   hostFsWrite=true]           read=  write=  total=23  ✅
[W-BOTH-FULL   allow+flag]                 read=  write=✓ edit=✓    ✅
[W-BOTH-SUBSET allow=[edit] + flag]        read=  write=  edit=✓    ✅
[M-FULL        read+write allow+both flags] read=✓ write=✓ edit=    ✅
[RCE-ATTEMPT   allow=exec,process,shell,spawn + both flags]
                                            exec= process= shell= spawn=  ✅

=== ALL PASS === (10/10)
```

**Key results:**

- **W-BOTH-FULL** ✅ — both keys set + all write names in allow → `write` and `edit` are reachable. (`apply_patch` correctly absent — factory doesn't produce it yet, deferred for future.)
- **W-BOTH-SUBSET** ✅ — `allow: ["edit"]` + `hostFsWrite: true` → only `edit` reachable; `write` correctly absent because not in allow. Per-tool granularity verified.
- **W-ALLOW-ONLY** ✅ — bot-P1 critical case: legacy config with `allow: ["write", "edit", "apply_patch"]` alone does NOT grant write access on direct-invoke. The new `hostFsWrite: true` opt-in is required additionally.
- **RCE-ATTEMPT** ✅ — even with `allow: ["exec", "process", "shell", "spawn"]` AND both `hostFsRead` + `hostFsWrite` set, the RCE-class tools NEVER appear in the candidate set. This is structural (the materialization block only includes coding tool names, never RCE tools), not policy-filtered.

## Code-level test coverage

`src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` (new `describe` block for host_write_allow):

- ✅ fires (warn) when both keys set + `allow: ["write"]` on loopback
- ✅ fires for `allow: ["edit", "apply_patch"]` + `hostFsWrite: true`
- ✅ escalates to critical when bind is lan
- ✅ does NOT fire when only `allow: ["write"]` (legacy shape)
- ✅ does NOT fire when only `hostFsWrite: true`
- ✅ does NOT fire when `hostFsWrite: true` + `allow: ["read"]` (cross-class — different opt-in needed)

## Why a single fresh commit (not rebase of prior 5)

The original PR's 5 commits implemented a single-key `allow: ["exec", "process"]` opt-in with an `ownerOnly: true` metadata tag that the bot correctly flagged as DEAD CODE after upstream commit `02182d5a3031` removed the owner-only enforcement helper. Patching on top of those 5 commits to fix the bot's [P1] would have layered design-on-design. Cleaner to reset to #85664's head and write the redesign as one commit.

Backup of prior PR history retained at `backup/63919-pre-rebase-20260603-onto-85664` on the public fork.

## Why exec/process/spawn/shell are deferred

The bot's prior [P1] on this PR ("Enforce the exec/process owner gate") flagged that with upstream's removal of sender-owner gating, an `ownerOnly: true` tag is metadata-only — a trusted-proxy caller with `operator.write` can still reach allowlisted `exec`/`process` without owner check.

A `directInvoke.commandExec: true` flag (analogous to `hostFsWrite`) is **not sufficient** for the RCE-class because:
- `operator.write` is broadly distributed in trusted-proxy setups
- RCE-class needs `operator.admin` or `senderIsOwner` enforcement at the direct-invoke layer
- This requires threading principal context through `invokeGatewayTool` in `tools-invoke-shared.ts` — a separate refactor

That refactor belongs in its own PR with its own review surface. This PR ships the safe write extension and explicitly documents the deferral.

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Extend #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` over Gateway direct-invoke surfaces, using the same dual-key gating pattern (`gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` plus per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow`). Addresses ClawSweeper [P1] on the prior PR head (the dead `ownerOnly` tag); RCE-class tools (exec/process/spawn/shell) remain unavailable and are deferred to a follow-up PR.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `git checkout f31c56eeaf && docker run --rm -v $PWD:/work -w /work --platform linux/arm64 --memory=12g node:22-bookworm-slim bash -c 'set -e; corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@11.2.2 --activate && node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs && node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs && node /work/.proof-driver-63919.mjs'` — exits 0 with `=== ALL PASS ===` (10/10).

**Real environment tested:** built fresh from current PR head `f31c56eeaf` inside a clean throwaway `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container; workspace bind-mounted; produced `dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js`. Driver imports `resolveGatewayScopedTools` from that bundle and probes 10 config combinations.

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture, redacted):**
```
[bundle] /work/dist/tool-resolution-Blc5CWzR.js
[resolve] resolveGatewayScopedTools found

[R-VANILLA: no read keys]
  config: {}
  coding-tools present: []
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 23

[R-BOTH: allow:['read'] + hostFsRead:true]
  config: {"allow":["read"],"hostFsRead":true}
  coding-tools present: [read]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 24

[W-BOTH-FULL: allow:['write','edit','apply_patch'] + hostFsWrite:true]
  config: {"allow":["write","edit","apply_patch"],"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [write, edit]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 25

[W-BOTH-SUBSET: allow:['edit'] + hostFsWrite:true (only edit reachable)]
  config: {"allow":["edit"],"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [edit]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 24

[M-FULL: allow:['read','write'] + both flags]
  config: {"allow":["read","write"],"hostFsRead":true,"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: [read, write]
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 25

[RCE-ATTEMPT: allow:['exec','process','shell','spawn'] + both flags]
  config: {"allow":["exec","process","shell","spawn"],"hostFsRead":true,"hostFsWrite":true}
  coding-tools present: []
  RCE present (must be empty): []
  total tools: 23

=== ALL PASS === (10/10)
```

**Observed result after fix (head `f31c56eeaf`):** All 10 cases PASS. The bot's prior [P1] dead-code concern is structurally addressed by the new design: write-class tools are dual-key gated; RCE-class tools are explicitly NOT exposed (and the proof verifies they cannot leak even when an operator deliberately tries with all gates open).

**What was not tested:** Live HTTP request through `handleToolsInvokeHttp` to a running gateway. The `resolveGatewayScopedTools` driver tests the candidate-set construction (where the dual-key gating + structural RCE non-exposure live); downstream policy filter + HTTP dispatch are upstream-tested code paths.




## Live HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` proof

Addresses ClawSweeper's [P1]: "Add redacted current-head HTTP and SDK RPC proof for denied and enabled read/write/edit cases."

Built `dist/` fresh from head `f31c56eeaf` in `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container, started the gateway via `node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 18900 --auth token --token <secret>`, mutated `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` per case, and POST'd `/tools/invoke` with node fetch targeting sentinel files. Stop + restart between cases.

**Results — 6 / 6 PASS:**

```
====================================================
LIVE HTTP /tools/invoke proof for PR #63919 (write/edit)
head: f31c56eeaf
====================================================

R-CASE 4 (regression): read with both keys
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["read"] hostFsRead=true hostFsWrite=false
  HTTP 200 body: {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"PROOF READ source for #63919\n"}]}}

W-CASE 1: neither write gate (default-deny)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=[] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=false
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

W-CASE 2: only allow=[write] (legacy)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["write"] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=false
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

W-CASE 3: only hostFsWrite=true
  [mutate_cfg] allow=[] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=true
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}

W-CASE 4: BOTH (success expected)
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["write","edit"] hostFsRead=false hostFsWrite=true
  HTTP 200 body: {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 22 bytes to /tmp/proof-write-target.txt"}]}}

RCE-CASE: exec with all flags + allow=[exec]
  [mutate_cfg] allow=["exec"] hostFsRead=true hostFsWrite=true
  HTTP 404 body: {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: exec"}}

write target file contents (proves W-CASE 4 actually wrote):
PROOF WRITE for #63919
```

**Critical observations:**

- **W-CASE 2** (legacy `allow:["write"]` only) returns HTTP **404 "Tool not available: write"** — same as the default-deny W-CASE 1. The dual-key gating prevents the bot's [P1] concern: existing configs that already include `"write"` in `allow` (for an unrelated surface) stay inert here, no silent host-FS write grant on upgrade.
- **W-CASE 4** (BOTH `hostFsWrite: true` + `allow:["write","edit"]`) returns HTTP **200** AND **actually wrote** the file (`PROOF WRITE for #63919` is the verbatim file content after the request).
- **RCE-CASE** demonstrates structural RCE non-exposure: even with `allow:["exec"]` PLUS both opt-in flags set, `exec` returns HTTP **404 "Tool not available: exec"**. The resolver does not materialize RCE-class tools into the direct-invoke candidate set; this is structural, not policy-filtered. Deferred to a follow-up PR with proper owner-gate enforcement.

## apply_patch alignment (ClawSweeper [P2])

Resolved the docs/audit/resolver mismatch flagged by ClawSweeper:

- `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md`: truth table now lists `{write, edit}` only; explicit note that `apply_patch` is in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for future-proofing but the coding tool factory does not produce it for direct-invoke yet.
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts`: `WRITE_CLASS_TOOLS` reduced to `{write, edit}` (was `{write, edit, apply_patch}`). The `host_write_allow` audit no longer falsely claims host-write exposure for an inert `allow: ["apply_patch"]` entry.

`apply_patch` can be added in a follow-up PR when upstream wires the factory entry.

## Docs discoverability (ClawSweeper [P2])

Same surfaces as #85664, extended for `hostFsWrite`:

- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — FIELD_HELP entries for `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsRead` AND `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite`.
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` — paragraph for each opt-in, with audit-warning hookup.
- `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — catalog rows for `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_read_allow` AND `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow`.
